steps.title: Use vertex form
steps.content: The maximum height is $27.04$ feet at $t=1.3$, so the vertex is $(1.3,27.04)$. A quadratic with this vertex can be written as $$f(t)=a(t-1.3)^2+27.04$$

steps.title: Use the launch height
steps.content: The object is launched from ground level, so $f(0)=0$. Substitute $t=0$: $$0=a(0-1.3)^2+27.04$$ $$0=1.69a+27.04$$ $$a=-16$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The model is $$f(t)=-16(t-1.3)^2+27.04$$ which matches choice A.
